title: Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act aims to support and enable small AI innovation businesses. The specifics of the policy remain unknown due to the lack of a description.

title: To establish a pilot program for use by U.S. Customs and Border Protection at land ports of entry along the Arizona border to assess the use of artificial intelligence through an anomaly detection algorithm, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Homeland Security.
summary: The U.S. government plans to launch a pilot program using artificial intelligence at Arizona land borders to detect unusual activity. This program, managed by U.S. Customs and Border Protection, aims to assess the effectiveness of the technology in its role.

title: CREATE AI Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The CREATE AI Act of 2025 is an unnamed policy that aims to outline a framework for artificial intelligence, but its specific goals and details are currently unknown.

title: Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.
summary: The Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026 aims to establish rules preventing the use of AI to commit electoral fraud. The law likely focuses on ensuring the integrity of elections by regulating the use of AI technologies within the electoral process.

title: Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: The Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence Act is a policy document that likely concerns the creation or regulation of a government department handling advanced technology and artificial intelligence. It may outline the responsibilities, powers, and goals of this department.

title: To amend the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 to require the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy to establish a prize competition program relating to artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This document proposes an update to the 2020 National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act, requiring a prize competition program on artificial intelligence. The update will establish a program to promote innovation and achievement in the field of artificial intelligence.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act is a proposed law that likely aims to temporarily or permanently halt the establishment of new artificial intelligence data centers.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 aims to regulate and mitigate the negative environmental effects caused by the growing use of artificial intelligence. This act likely addresses concerns related to AI's potential environmental impact, such as increased energy consumption and e-waste generation.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 aims to guide the usage of artificial intelligence within the Small Business Administration. The act will provide guidelines and regulations to support the development and implementation of AI systems across SBA operations.

title: American Leadership in AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committees on Energy and Commerce, Agriculture, Oversight and Government Reform, Education and Workforce, the Judiciary, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The American Leadership in AI Act is a US policy aiming to promote American leadership in the field of Artificial Intelligence. The policy does not provide detailed information on its specific objectives or measures, but its name suggests an intention to advance the United States' competitiveness and influence in AI.

title: Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy aims to promote transparency in consumer interactions with AI systems by requiring companies to disclose when such technologies are involved. The Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act likely allows companies to choose whether or not to participate in this disclosure pilot.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Forwarded by Subcommittee to Full Committee (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: This law aims to protect families by reducing AI data center energy costs. The goal is to make AI technology more affordable for households by decreasing expenses associated with its power consumption.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 is an act that aims to address and mitigate the environmental impacts caused by the development and deployment of artificial intelligence technologies. Its purpose is to regulate the use of AI in a way that minimizes harm to the environment.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 aims to establish a framework for governing artificial intelligence across various sectors. The law likely contains regulations and guidelines to ensure the safe and responsible development of AI technologies.

title: Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025 is a policy that aims to regulate and hold accountable companies using artificial intelligence and algorithms in their decision-making processes. This act likely seeks to increase transparency, fairness, and safety of these technologies by implementing strict guidelines and oversight mechanisms.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026 is a policy that aims to manage risks associated with AI technology. The act likely establishes guidelines and regulations to mitigate potential negative impacts of AI on society.

title: A bill to address the effects of artificial intelligence-enabled systems, including artificial intelligence chatbots, on older adults, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This bill aims to address the effects of artificial intelligence on older adults. It focuses on AI-enabled systems, including chatbots, for their intended purpose.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act aims to create a supportive regulatory environment for the development and use of artificial intelligence in the financial services industry. This act likely seeks to balance AI innovation with necessary safeguards to protect consumers and prevent potential risks associated with AI in finance.

title: China AI Power Report Act
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 47 - 0.
summary: The China AI Power Report Act appears to be a policy document related to the promotion and regulation of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in China. However, without more information, the specifics of the Act remain unclear.

title: Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026 aims to regulate and oversee the development of artificial intelligence in a responsible manner. The act likely seeks to mitigate potential risks and negative consequences of AI while promoting its safe and beneficial use.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a policy document that aims to create regulatory guidelines for the development and use of artificial intelligence models. However, without more information, the actual scope and specifics of this legislation are unknown.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Workforce for AI Trust Act is a policy aimed at establishing trust in the development and deployment of artificial intelligence. Its specific goals and measures are unclear without further information.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: The K-12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026 aims to prepare students in elementary and high school for a world with increasing artificial intelligence usage. This act likely focuses on integrating AI education into school curricula to ensure students are aware and skillful in AI technologies.

title: Preventing AI Censorship Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Preventing AI Censorship Act is a bill aimed at preventing the censorship of artificial intelligence and its applications. It likely seeks to establish regulations or guidelines to protect AI from unjustified suppression or control by governments or other entities.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: The Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act aims to assess and mitigate potential risks associated with the use of generative AI technology. The Act is likely to establish guidelines and regulations to prevent misuse of this technology for terrorist purposes.

title: A bill to prohibit certain uses of automated decision systems by employers, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This bill aims to limit employers' use of automated decision-making systems, and it covers additional unspecified purposes. The policy may regulate how employers use technology to make decisions about their employees or applicants.

title: A bill to amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to impose an excise tax on systemically important AI activity, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.
summary: This bill proposes to implement an excise tax on AI systems deemed "systemically important" by the government. The tax is intended to raise revenue and possibly regulate the use of artificial intelligence.

title: Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026 aims to regulate the development and use of artificial intelligence in military operations to ensure security and accountability. This policy likely includes provisions for oversight, transparency, and potential consequences for misuse of AI in military contexts.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act aims to safeguard consumers from misleading or false information presented by artificial intelligence. The act's purpose is to establish guidelines and regulations for the development and use of AI to prevent consumer deception.
